event.prompt_text: Explain how a royalty-driven LLM should make answer sources trustworthy.
event.response_text: A royalty-driven LLM should verify that cited sources are real, metadata-faithful, and claim-supporting before placing them in the answer footer.

claims.claim_text: Citation identity must reject fabricated or metadata-swapped citations before they appear in the public answer footer.

citations.title: Citation Identity for Grounded AI Attribution
citations.quoted_text: Private verifier excerpt: citation hallucinations include fabricated, metadata-swapped citations that should not enter a public answer footer.

citations.title: Synthetic Images and Attribution Markets
citations.quoted_text: Private verifier excerpt: unrelated text about synthetic image markets.

citations.title: A Nonexistent Study About Perfect LLM Citations
citations.quoted_text: Private verifier excerpt: this fabricated citation has no authority record.

authority_records.canonical_title: Citation Identity for Grounded AI Attribution
authority_records.abstract: This authority record studies citation hallucinations in LLM answers and requires detection of fabricated citations, metadata-swapped citations, and claim-support failures before a source can appear in a public answer footer.
